title: Sysmon Lateral Movement via SMB
description: Detects SMB connections (port 445) from processes commonly used for lateral movement. Attackers use PsExec, WMI, and other tools to move laterally through SMB/Windows Admin Shares.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and (('Destination Port' = 445 and Initiated = 'true') and (('Process Name' like '*\psexec.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\psexesvc.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\PSEXESVC.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\cmd.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\powershell.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\wmic.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\net.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\net1.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\xcopy.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\robocopy.exe')))
